steps.title: Use the isosceles condition
steps.content: A triangle is isosceles if it has at least two equal side lengths. In a triangle, equal angles are opposite equal sides, so two equal angles are sufficient.

steps.title: Match the given angle
steps.content: Angle $U$ is $31^\circ$. If angle $V$ is also $31^\circ$, then angles $U$ and $V$ are equal, so the triangle must be isosceles. The answer is D.
